audit_items.proof_trace: When nfp_modify_flow_metadata() fails at L1526-1528 (returns -ENOENT if stats_ctx_table entry is missing), execution jumps directly to err_free_merge_flow (L1548), bypassing the nfp_tunnel_del_ipv4_off() (L1531) and nfp_tunnel_put_ipv6_off() (L1534) calls. These functions decrement reference counts of globally-tracked tunnel IP address entries (nfp_ipv4_addr_entry / nfp_ipv6_addr_entry in ipv4_off_list / ipv6_off_list); skipping them leaves ref counts elevated, preventing those entries from ever being freed even after the flow is removed. The action_data, mask_data, unmasked_data, and nfp_flow struct itself are correctly freed at err_free_merge_flow.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A TC flower offload delete operation on a flow with tunnel addresses configured, where the stats_ctx_table entry is missing or corrupt (causing nfp_modify_flow_metadata to return -ENOENT), causes the global tunnel IP address entries to accumulate with inflated ref counts — a kernel memory leak exploitable via repeated add/delete cycles to exhaust kernel memory.
